forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY2CX5RR49YNTTKQXG2KHXCD
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+title: Document IgnoreProcessor in README and note the plan.md scope extension
+---
+## What
+Documentation follow-on, split from the demo task to keep concerns separate.
+
+1. Update `README.md` with a short IgnoreProcessor section: construction from any file name, `+` combination (`.gitignore` + `.reviewignore` example), and `evaluate` returning verdicts with why (show a sample verdict description string). The example snippet must mirror a real compiled test case in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/IgnoreProcessorTests.swift` (same API calls, same expected verdict text) so the docs cannot silently rot — name the mirrored test in a comment in the test file
+2. `plan.md` describes three pillars; add a one-line note there that IgnoreProcessor is an addition beyond the original three-pillar plan, so the project's own architecture doc does not silently diverge
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] README section exists with construction, `+`, and evaluate-with-why examples whose API calls match a named, passing test case
+- [ ] plan.md notes the scope extension in one line
+- [ ] No other README/plan.md content reworded or restructured
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] The mirrored test case in `IgnoreProcessorTests.swift` exists, is named in both files, and passes via `swift test --filter IgnoreProcessorTests`
+- [ ] Full `swift test` stays green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — the mirrored test exists before the README snippet that cites it.
\ No newline at end of file
